Given: A user has multiple tabs open
When: The user closes all tabs except one and logs in to that single tab
Then: Login completes successfully, OTP is delivered without issues, and secure session is established

Given: A user is logged in to one tab
When: The user duplicates the tab to create multiple instances of the same session
Then: All duplicated tabs maintain the user's session and provide full access without re-authentication

Scenario_Name: Session Token Sharing Across Duplicated Tabs
Given: A user has logged in and duplicated the tab multiple times
When: The user navigates to different sections of the application across multiple tabs
Then: All tabs share the same session token and maintain synchronized session state

Scenario_Name: Single Tab Logout Impact on Duplicated Tabs
Given: A user is logged in with multiple duplicated tabs active
When: The user clicks logout on one of the duplicated tabs
Then: System behavior is consistent: either all tabs logout or only the current tab logs out (design-dependent)

Scenario_Name: Multiple Simultaneous Login Attempts - Same Account
Given: A user has multiple tabs open and is attempting to login from each tab
When: OTP is triggered for each tab attempting to login simultaneously
Then: System handles gracefully without failing OTP delivery; provides clear guidance or error messages

Scenario_Name: OTP Delivery with Multiple Pending Login Attempts
Given: A user is attempting to login from 3+ tabs at the same time
When: OTP delivery is triggered for each concurrent login attempt
Then: System delivers OTP correctly without confusion; only one valid session is created after OTP verification

Given: A user is logged in through duplicated tabs and system session state is modified in one tab
When: The user performs an action in another tab that depends on session state
Then: The system either maintains consistency or handles state mismatch gracefully with a warning

Given: A user is logged in with multiple active tabs
When: The user closes some tabs but keeps others open
Then: Remaining tabs continue to maintain the user's session without interruption

Given: A user is logged in and viewing different pages on multiple tabs
When: The user switches between tabs and continues working
Then: Session remains active and consistent; user data is current across all tabs

Given: A user has multiple duplicated tabs with active sessions
When: The user refreshes one of the duplicated tabs
Then: The refreshed tab maintains the session and does not require re-authentication

Given: A user is already logged in to one tab
When: The user opens a new tab and navigates to the application
Then: The new tab either maintains the session (if cookies/tokens allow) or requires re-authentication per design

Given: A user is logged in one regular tab and opens a private/incognito tab
When: The user attempts to access the application on the private tab
Then: The private tab should require separate authentication as sessions are isolated by browser design

Given: User A is logged in on one tab, attempting to login as User B on another tab
Then: System should prevent simultaneous active sessions or clearly manage separate sessions without cross-contamination

Given: A user is logged in with multiple tabs showing form with file upload
When: The user uploads a file on one tab while accessing the system on another tab
Then: File uploads are processed independently; session is maintained on both tabs without conflicts

Given: User has multiple tabs open; session timeout is triggered on one tab due to inactivity
When: The user switches to another tab and attempts to perform an action
Then: System detects expired session and either expands timeout across all tabs or requires re-login (per design)

Given: A user completes login on one tab and browser history contains pre-login pages
When: The user clicks the back button multiple times on a duplicated tab
Then: System prevents navigation back to login page and maintains secure session state

Given: A user has multiple tabs open and a modal dialog appears on one tab
When: The user switches to another tab and then back
Then: Modal state is preserved or handled appropriately; session continues normally

Scenario_Name: Session Conflict - One Tab Logs Out User Globally
Given: A user has multiple tabs active; one tab logs out the user
When: Other tabs attempt to access protected resources
Then: Remaining tabs either show logout confirmation or attempt a graceful redirect to login based on system design
